titleEn: New retrofit spot spraying system for every type of sprayer
titleZh: 新型改装式定点喷雾系统适配各类喷雾机
summaryZh: 德国初创公司DynamoBot推出WeSee定点喷雾系统，通过为每个喷嘴安装电子控制阀，实现对杂草的单独开关喷雾。该系统可加装在几乎所有类型的田间喷雾机上，包括新旧机型，并采用RGB摄像头和近红外传感器进行杂草识别。作为波恩大学和PhenoRob的衍生企业，DynamoBot的技术为精准杂草管理提供了高性价比的改装方案。
featuredReason: 该技术展示了AI视觉与传感器在精准农业中的低成本改装方案，对智慧畜牧行业中的种植饲料作物管理具有参考价值，体现了物联网与自动化在田间作业中的实际应用。
contentFull: Buyers guides Field robots Autosteer retrofit kits Autonomous Retrofit Kits Autonomous Tractors Drones Smart weeders Tech in focus Autonomoussemi-auto steering systems Field robots Drones Smart farming Indoor farming Tools & data Sustainability Crop solutions Fertilizing Tillage WeedPest control Irrigation Harvest & storage Digital magazine Newsletter Our sites Subscribe LoginRegister Subscribe Partners Newsletter Subscribe ![Image 2: login!Image 3: logout](https:www.futurefarming.comcrop-solutionsweed-pest-controlnew-retrofit-spot-spraying-system-for-every-type-of-sprayer) ![Image 4: Future Farming](https:www.futurefarming.com) Buyers guides Field robots Autonomous Tractors Autonomous Retrofit Kits Autosteer Retrofit Kits Drones Smart weeders Tech in focus Drones Field robots Autonomoussemi-auto steering systems Crop solutions Fertilizing Tillage WeedPest control Irrigation Harvest & storage Smart farming Indoor farming Tools & data Sustainability ![Image 5](https:www.futurefarming.comcrop-solutionsweed-pest-controlnew-retrofit-spot-spraying-system-for-every-type-of-sprayer) Logout Gateway to the world of smart farming New retrofit spot spraying system for every type of sprayer 22-06 | WeedPest control | News LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint !Image 6: DynamoBot supplies the WeSee spot spraying system with individual nozzle control, which can be mounted on almost any type of field sprayer; new, relatively new and considerably older machines. Photo's: René Koerhuis DynamoBot supplies the WeSee spot spraying system with individual nozzle control, which can be mounted on almost any type of field sprayer; new, relatively new and considerably older machines. Photo's: René Koerhuis The WeSee setup from German company DynamoBot initially appears to be ‘yet another new spot spraying system’. Until a number of distinctive features become apparent. Startup DynamoBot has been working on solutions for precision weed management since 2023 as a spin-off from the University of Bonn and PhenoRob. Other now well-known PhenoRob spin-offs include laser weeder manufacturer Escarda Technologies and drone services provider Pheno-Inspect. One of DynamoBot’s developments is the WeSee spot spraying system with individual nozzle control, which can be mounted on almost any type of field sprayer; new, relatively new and considerably older machines. According to the company, this makes its technology unique. !Image 7: By fitting each nozzle holder with an electronically controlled valve, each nozzle can be switched on and off individually to spot spray weeds. By fitting each nozzle holder with an electronically controlled valve, each nozzle can be switched on and off individually to spot spray weeds. RGB camera and NIR sensor With a background in research mainly into applications of vision technology in agriculture, the company developed its retrofit spot spraying system. To do this, it mounts a camera set every 2.5 metres on an existing spray boom, consisting of a visual RGB camera and a NIR sensor. The current camera set also still contains the ECU, which is due to be moved to the machine itself in the coming period so that the cameras become considerably more compact and lighter. The cameras scan the field and can detect weeds on bare land (green-on-brown) and in a crop (green-on-green). By fitting each nozzle holder with an electronically controlled valve, the cameras and underlying algorithms can then switch each nozzle on and off individually to spot spray weeds. Thanks to the NIR sensors, this is also possible in little or no daylight. Existing nozzles can continue to be used; pulse-width modulation nozzles (PWM) are not required. Setup and operation are via a supplied tablet. At present, algorithms and crop models are available for potatoes, maize and sugar beet, and these are being tested on the arable farm of one of the employees. !Image 8: Existing nozzles can continue to be used; pulse-width modulation nozzles (PWM) are not required. Existing nozzles can continue to be used; pulse-width modulation nozzles (PWM) are not required. Self-installation also possible The system is suitable for nozzle spacings of 25 and 50 centimetres and should ultimately be simple enough to install yourself. DynamoBot offers the WeSee system for €2,000 per metre of spray boom. For the time being, there are no additional annual costs, but these will be introduced. For crop models other than potatoes, maize and sugar beet, the company charges €500 per crop. WeSee is commercially available, but at DLG Feldtage the first sale had yet to be made. In addition to spot spraying crop and weed plants, the system can also spot spray all green plants on arable land and, of course, also work across the full field. Thanks to the electronically controlled nozzle holders, installation also provides individual nozzle control. translationZh:
德国公司DynamoBot推出的WeSee系统初看似乎只是“又一套新的定点喷雾系统”，直到其若干独特特点显现出来。

初创公司DynamoBot自2023年起作为波恩大学和PhenoRob的衍生企业，一直致力于精准杂草管理解决方案的研发。其他目前知名的PhenoRob衍生企业包括激光除草机制造商Escarda Technologies和无人机服务提供商Pheno-Inspect。

DynamoBot的成果之一是WeSee定点喷雾系统，该系统具有独立喷嘴控制功能，可加装在几乎所有类型的田间喷雾机上——无论是新机器、较新的机器还是相当老旧的机器。据该公司称，这使其技术独具特色。

**RGB摄像头与近红外传感器**

凭借主要研究农业视觉技术应用的背景，该公司开发了这款改装式定点喷雾系统。为此，他们在现有喷雾吊杆上每隔2.5米安装一套摄像头组，该摄像头组由可见光RGB摄像头和近红外传感器组成。
contentHtml: The WeSee setup from German company DynamoBot initially appears to be ‘yet another new spot spraying system’. Until a number of distinctive features become apparent.
